summary: Menu subsystem decompilation work focused on previously 0% matched mn files. The PR added implementations and headers for mnevent and mnitemsw, added mncount functions and data structs, updated mnmain animation-loop helper typing to AnimLoopSettings, and propagated related typed call-site changes across several mn files. Bot output reported 15 new matches and 5 unmatched-item improvements; reviewer approved and stated AnimLoopSettings makes sense.

key_files.role: Added many event-menu function bodies, static AnimLoopSettings data, JObj animation handling, text cleanup, menu state transitions, and event initialization code.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: mnevent.c added mnEvent_8024CE74, mnEvent_8024D014, mnEvent_8024D0CC, mnEvent_8024D4E0, mnEvent_8024D7E0, mnEvent_8024E420, mnEvent_8024E838, fn_8024E1B4, fn_8024E2A0, fn_8024E34C
bot_report: mnevent new matches and improvements

evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: MnEventData and typed mnEvent prototypes

key_files.role: Added item-switch menu functions for JObj lookup and menu entry/setup.
evidence_refs: bot_report: mnItemSw_8023405C and mnItemSw_802358C0 new matches

key_files.role: Added include guard, MnItemSwData layout, typed prototypes, and extern for mnItemSw_804D6BEC.
Added mnCount_802513F4 and mnCount_802517E0, using MnCountData fields, JObj hide/show flags, loops for text arrays, and AnimLoopSettings.
evidence_refs: bot_report: mnCount_802513F4 and mnCount_802517E0 new matches

evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: mn_8022EC18 signature and start_frame/end_frame/loop_frame field use

Converted two float[3] animation settings to AnimLoopSettings and updated call sites to field access.
Updated mn_8022EC18 calls to pass AnimLoopSettings objects rather than address of start_frame.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: mnDeflicker_803EEFE4 and mnDeflicker_803EEFD8 call-site changes

key_files.role: Updated mn_8022EC18 call sites with AnimLoopSettings casts while retaining existing x/y field checks.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: casts to AnimLoopSettings* for mnLanguage_803EF5AC and mnLanguage_803EF5A0

what_changed: Implemented substantial portions of src/melee/mn/mnevent.c from an empty/near-empty file, including event-count calculation, JObj visibility updates, animation transitions, text cleanup, and menu-entry setup.
Added typed menu data structs MnEventData, MnItemSwData, and MnCountData in their owning headers.
Replaced several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with concrete signatures for recovered mn event, item-switch, and count functions.
Changed mn_8022EC18 to accept AnimLoopSettings* and use start_frame, end_frame, and loop_frame fields instead of indexing a float[3].
Updated related mn call sites in mndeflicker, mnhyaku, mnlanguage, and mnmain to match the AnimLoopSettings-oriented helper signature.
Used natural loops to initialize or clean repeated arrays in MnEventData and MnCountData.
Bot report recorded +2768 matched code bytes, 15 new 100% matched items, and 5 improvements in unmatched items.

smart_moves.text: Typed recovered function prototypes in headers alongside function body recovery, reducing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS drift.
Recovered concrete per-menu data structs to express user_data fields directly rather than leaving raw offsets in the shown implementations.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: MnEventData fields used in mnevent.c
diff_excerpt: MnItemSwData fields used in mnitemsw.c
diff_excerpt: MnCountData fields used in mncount.c

smart_moves.text: Applied a subsystem-wide helper type cleanup for mn_8022EC18 call sites after changing its signature to AnimLoopSettings*.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: mndeflicker.c, mnhyaku.c, mnlanguage.c call-site updates

smart_moves.text: Used compact loops for repeated array initialization and cleanup, matching the decomp standard preference for natural loop forms over repeated blocks.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: mnCount_802517E0 for loop over labels and values
diff_excerpt: mnEvent_8024E420 for loop over gobjs, texts, icons
diff_excerpt: fn_8024E1B4 for loop cleanup over gobjs, texts, icons

decomp_lessons.lesson: When recovering menu user_data access, introduce a local data struct once field roles are supported by multiple accesses in the implementation; this improves signatures and avoids raw pointer arithmetic.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: MnEventData with page, first_event, gobjs, texts, icons, name_text, desc_text
diff_excerpt: MnItemSwData with menu_kind, cursor, items, jobjs
diff_excerpt: MnCountData with cursor, page_size, labels, values, title

decomp_lessons.lesson: Three-float animation setting tuples used by mn animation helpers may be better represented as AnimLoopSettings when call sites consistently use start, end, and loop-frame semantics.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: mnhyaku float[3] converted to AnimLoopSettings
review: ribbanya approved with 'I think `AnimLoopSettings` does make sense.'
decomp_lessons.caveat: Author explicitly said they were suspicious of the AnimLoopSettings changes; reviewer approval supports this PR but does not establish a global rule for all float[3] data.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When repeated menu arrays are cleared or initialized together, a single counted loop over parallel arrays can be both readable and match-friendly.

naming_conventions.text: Recovered structs use subsystem-prefixed PascalCase names such as MnEventData, MnItemSwData, and MnCountData, while unclear functions keep address-style names such as fn_8024E1B4 and mnEvent_8024E838.
Local variables use short role names where roles are clear, including gobj, jobj, tree, data, proc, text, frame, count, and i.
Struct fields use semantic menu roles when apparent, such as cursor, page_size, labels, values, first_event, desc_text, name_text, items, and jobjs; less-supported byte fields remain x/pad-style.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: MnItemSwData fields x21, pad, x23

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK and FORCE_PAD_STACK macros were used narrowly in recovered functions to satisfy stack/codegen shape.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: FORCE_PAD_STACK_4 in mnCount_802513F4 and mnEvent_8024D0CC
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The PR slice shows bot match results but not per-function objdiff traces explaining each padding macro.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Kept a volatile load when requesting the start frame in mn_8022EC18, now expressed as *(volatile float*) &arg1->start_frame.
Used explicit casts to AnimLoopSettings* for mnlanguage call sites where existing data still appeared as vector-like x/y fields.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The loaded PR evidence does not explain whether these casts are a temporary bridge or final desired typing.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: For resource loading in mnEvent_8024E838, used a char* base into nearby static data and pointer offsets for lbArchive_LoadSections arguments.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: mnEvent_8024E838 char* base = (char*) &mnEvent_803EF740 and base + offsets
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: This may be data-layout-sensitive; no review discussion in the slice explains data ownership or section-layout reasoning.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Menu user_data structs can replace raw access in recovered mn functions
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2312 recovered MnEventData, MnItemSwData, and MnCountData and then used their fields directly in new matched menu functions. This is a graph-safe example of typed-fields-over-pointer-math within the mn subsystem.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: struct typedefs and field uses
bot_report: functions using these structs listed as new matches

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep unclear function names address-style while typing signatures
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2312 typed many recovered prototypes but retained fn_/address-style names for functions whose semantics were not renamed in the evidence.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: fn_8024D864, fn_8024E1B4, fn_8024E2A0, fn_8024E34C, fn_80233E10

source_update_candidates.title: Treat AnimLoopSettings as a candidate type for three-frame mn animation tuples
source_update_candidates.text: In PR #2312, mn_8022EC18 was changed from float* indexing to AnimLoopSettings* field access, with related call-site updates in mndeflicker, mnhyaku, mnlanguage, and mnmain. Reviewer feedback said AnimLoopSettings makes sense, while the author noted initial suspicion. Candidate standard should be scoped cautiously: use AnimLoopSettings when local call sites and data semantics support start_frame/end_frame/loop_frame; do not cast unrelated vector-like data solely to satisfy a helper signature without local evidence.
evidence_refs: author body: suspicious of AnimLoopSettings changes
source_update_candidates.reason: This may be reusable for mn animation helper typing, but evidence includes uncertainty and should not become an unconditional rule.

source_update_candidates.title: mnmainrule details need full diff before curation
source_update_candidates.text: PR metadata and bot report show mnmainrule.c changed and gained new matches for mn_80231714, mn_8023164C, and mn_80231F80, but the loaded diff excerpt does not include those hunks.
source_update_candidates.reason: Possible path fact exists, but the intake slice lacks enough detail to summarize implementation tactics.

rejection_notes: Do not promote 'Claude confidence' as evidence; only the diff, bot report, and reviewer approval are usable PR evidence.
Do not infer full validation beyond the bot report; no local build logs or objdiff traces are included.
Do not generalize PAD_STACK/FORCE_PAD_STACK use as style; the slice shows their presence and bot results but not the exact necessity for each function.
Do not infer detailed changes for mnsound, mnsoundtest, mnvibration, or mnmainrule beyond changed-file metadata unless the full diff is inspected.
